<!-- generated by session_final_report for h5bench/20260710_061131 at 2026-07-11 00:22 UTC -->

## Session narrative

Continued an in-progress h5bench baseline-collection pipeline on Tuolumne. Initial state: 6 of
7 workloads already baselined; hdf5_iotest and exerciser needed calibration fixes (exerciser's
`--minels` value was catastrophically oversized; both were corrected and completed).

Mid-session, scope was narrowed by the user to `read` and `write` only. Verification of the
existing `read` baseline revealed it was silently broken (h5bench_read needs a pre-existing
file; the original scripts pointed it at an empty path, producing a silent HDF5 error-storm
that still "succeeded"). `write.cfg` was also found to be 16x oversized against the actual
`du`-verified output size, not just the misleading trace-log size. Both were fixed, recalibrated
via the single-process -> 1-node -> 8-node methodology with real `du` + real flux job elapsed
time checks (never trusting a byte-count projection alone), and re-baselined to 5 replicates
each.

A runaway job-submission-loop incident occurred mid-session (not caused by this agent — found
already in progress, reported immediately, resolved by the coordinator with scoped
per-allocation `flux cancel`, never a global cancel). This prompted a new mandatory
occupancy-check rule in the `flux-alloc` skill.

The optimization pass tested 7 candidate levers across L1 (app pattern — quickly ruled invalid
since changing access pattern changes what's measured, not a real optimization), L2 (software/
HDF5), L3 (filesystem/Lustre), network (Slingshot/MPI), and memory (NUMA/huge-pages) axes, each
literature-backed via targeted arXiv/Semantic Scholar searches (h5bench-, Tuolumne/Slingshot-,
Lustre-PFL-, and MI300A-specific, not just generic HDF5/MPI-IO). One lever (ROMIO
cb_nodes+striping) produced an unambiguous, comparator-corroborated ~5x-8x win. Three others
(network NIC/rendezvous tuning, NUMA affinity) produced large but ultimately noise-driven median
deltas — caught only because a same-rep `comparator` cross-check was run for every claimed
"win," not just the 5-rep median. Two levers (paged file-space, huge pages) were rejected before
any scale run due to fundamental incompatibilities/implementation gaps found in single-process
smoke tests, saving allocation time.

Every measured result — win, rejection, or inconclusive — was recorded to
`dftracer-optimization-kb` with honest verdicts in the notes field, including retracting an
earlier misleading rep1-vs-rep1 claim once the full 5-rep data contradicted it.
